SQLite Performance Tuning for Embedded Applications

By Maria Silva | November 20, 2023

SQLite is remarkably fast out of the box, but careful tuning can yield significant improvements for embedded use cases. This guide covers the most impactful PRAGMA settings and schema design patterns.

Essential PRAGMAs

The following PRAGMAs should be set at connection time:

PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
PRAGMA cache_size = -64000;  -- 64MB cache
PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;

Schema Design

Proper indexing is critical. Consider this schema for an article table:

CREATE TABLE articles (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    url TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    published_at DATETIME,
    content TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_articles_published
    ON articles(published_at DESC);

Benchmarks

Configuration Inserts/sec Reads/sec
Default 1,200 45,000
WAL + Normal sync 8,500 48,000
WAL + 64MB cache 8,500 62,000

Conclusion

With minimal configuration, SQLite can handle tens of thousands of operations per second, making it an excellent choice for applications like RSS readers that need fast local storage.
